step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the previous conclusion. The key issue highlighted was that while some high-copper silver alloys can have higher mercury content if improperly formulated, this isn’t necessarily true for all such alloys. Given the variability in formulation and production processes, assuming all high-copper silver alloys contain excessive amounts of mercury would be overly broad and inaccurate.

Therefore, my answer choice remains: B. Disagreeing with the notion that "It has less mercury content" as being universally applicable since there could still exist properly formulated versions free from elevated mercury levels. Answer Choice: B.
